one_line: An Indus crafts specialist transforming stone into export beads in the Mature Harappan era.

appearance.age_look: mid-20s, lean craftsperson’s build with strong forearms and steady hands
appearance.face: oval face with medium-brown Indus-valley complexion, straight nose, sharp dark eyes with slightly thick brows, a few tiny stone-dust freckles on the cheeks, calm focused expression that shifts to intense concentration while working
appearance.hair: straight black hair parted slightly off-center, pulled back into a neat low bun at the nape and bound with a narrow dark-brown cord, no headwrap, a single short wavy strand loose at the temple
appearance.costume: knee-length wrapped cotton tunic in undyed off‑white, V-neck and short sleeves, worn over a simple ankle-length cotton dhoti-style lower wrap in muted terracotta red, both secured with a plain brown woven waist sash; bare lower arms with a single narrow copper-bronze bangle on the right wrist, small carnelian bead necklace of alternating long barrel and tiny disc beads in deep orange-red, and simple leather thong sandals; fabric is plain-weave with very subtle narrow vertical stripes on the tunic only, no elaborate patterns
appearance.signature: a palm-sized flat stone drill-board held in the left hand, with one carnelian bead in progress and a thin dark chert micro-drill mounted in a small wooden bow-drill frame gripped in the right hand, its cord slightly taut as if mid-spin

ref_desc: A mid-20s Harappan bead maker with oval medium-brown face, black hair in a low bun, off-white tunic, terracotta dhoti, carnelian necklace, bronze wrist bangle, and leather sandals, holding a stone drill-board and bow-mounted chert micro-drill in deep carnelian-orange, brown, and off-white tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of a Mature Harappan bead maker: a lean mid-20s craftsperson with an oval medium-brown face, sharp dark eyes, and black hair parted slightly off-center and tied into a neat low bun with a dark brown cord, one small strand loose at the temple. They wear an off-white knee-length wrapped cotton tunic with a simple V-neck and short sleeves over an ankle-length terracotta-red dhoti-style lower wrap, both tied with a plain brown woven waist sash; the tunic fabric is plain-weave with only very subtle narrow vertical stripes, not decorative patterns. Their lower arms are bare except for a single narrow copper-bronze bangle on the right wrist, and around the neck is a small carnelian bead necklace of alternating long barrel and tiny disc beads in deep orange-red. On the feet are simple leather thong sandals. In their left hand they hold a flat stone drill-board with a single carnelian bead in progress, while the right hand grips a slim wooden bow-drill frame with a thin dark chert micro-drill point and taut cord, posed as if mid-spin. Use a palette dominated by off-white, terracotta red, deep carnelian orange-red, dark brown, and bronze, and show the character from multiple angles in the same outfit and hairstyle to emphasize consistent face, hair, costume, and tools.
